description: This selected area electron diffraction pattern (SADP) was captured from a coarse precipitate particle designated as κ′′ (kappa double-prime) located within the ferrite (α)/D03 matrix of as-cast Fe–10Al–xMn–1.0C alloys. The pattern exhibits distinct superlattice reflection spots indexed as 100, 110, 120, 200, 210, and 220, alongside the fundamental 000 reflection. The presence of these superlattice spots with uniform intensity confirms that the κ′′ particle possesses an ordered L12-type crystal structure (space group Pm3̄m), representing the (Fe,Mn)3AlCx carbide phase. The caption and citation context indicate this carbide has a lattice parameter of a = 0.387–0.389 nm and forms at high temperatures with a relatively random carbon distribution compared to finer κ carbides in the austenite matrix. This diffraction evidence establishes that κ′′ carbides within the D03 ferrite region are structurally distinct from the L′12-type κ/κ′ carbides precipitating in the austenite (γ) phase, supporting the duplex-phase microstructural model where coarse κ′′ particles strengthen the D03 matrix through a specific orientation relationship: [1̄11]κ′′ ∥ [220]D03 and (011)κ′′ ∥ (001)D03.
